title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
**Overview**
 Do you want to be at the forefront of innovating the latest hardware designs to propel Microsoft’s cloud growth? Are you seeking a unique career opportunity that combines technical capabilities, cross\\-team collaboration with business insight and strategy? This
 **Senior Software Engineer** 
 role with a focus on supporting hardware/software co\\-design and evaluation of novel systems architecture concepts to improve datacenter performance, efficiency, and reliability might be the right one for you.
   

  

 Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees, we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to achieve our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond. In alignment with our Microsoft values, we are committed to cultivating an inclusive work environment for all employees to positively impact our culture every day.
   

  

 Join the
 **Strategic Planning and Architecture (SPARC)** 
 team within Microsoft’s Azure Hardware Systems and Infrastructure (AHSI) organization, the team behind Microsoft’s expanding Cloud Infrastructure and for powering Microsoft’s “Intelligent Cloud” mission. Microsoft delivers more than 200 online services to more than one billion individuals worldwide, and AHSI is the team behind our expanding cloud infrastructure. We deliver the core infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's cloud businesses including Microsoft Azure, Bing, MSN, Office 365, OneDrive, Skype, Teams, and Xbox Live.
   

  

 As part of the Systems Planning and Architecture (SPARC) group, you will help with pathfinding and architecture for future compute platforms, storage and related technologies that create advantages for Azure and Microsoft. You will collaborate across the Azure organization to evaluate next\\-generation datacenter technologies and influence Azure product roadmaps for both Microsoft and 3rd party silicon and systems.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
 Responsibilities for this role span:
   

  

* Develop a deep understanding of workloads across key Azure segments and lead detailed performance evaluation of usage scenarios
* Orchestrate experimental setups to evaluate workload performance and inform architecture decisions for effective hardware/software co\\-design.
* Guide architecture for next\\-generation technologies through functional and performant PoC definitions and end\\-to\\-end prototyping.
* Support pathfinding to identify and quantify optimization opportunities with distributed and disaggregated system architectures.
* Support hardware validation and software enabling teams towards productization.


**Success In This Role Requires**
* Listening to Azure infrastructure experts and customers to identify current and emerging problems
* Identifying promising technologies to address problems that are relevant for Azure business, engage with ecosystem partners, and derisking productization via capable proof\\-of\\-concepts
* Working across organizational boundaries with roadmap planners, product architects, and hardware and software engineering teams to land promising solutions in Azure data centers.


**Qualifications**
 Required Qualifications
   

  

- 7\\+ years of technical engineering experience

* OR Bachelor’s degree in Electrical engineering, Computer Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field AND 10\\+ years of technical engineering experience
* OR Master’s degree in Electrical engineering, Computer Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field AND 6\\+ years of technical engineering experience
* OR Doctorate degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field AND 3\\+ years of technical engineering experience.
* 5\\+ years of experience with system performance evaluation using industry standard benchmarks and/or common cloud workloads
* 5\\+ years of experience with significant hardware/software co\\-design projects involving CPU and/or systems architecture and influencing technical direction.
* Intellectual curiosity and passion for learning and deploying new technologies.
* Verbal and written communication skills and ability to engage technical \\& non\\-technical peers
* Experience contributing to complex projects with respect and integrity, including those with multiple workstreams spanning different business and technical disciplines.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Deep expertise in CPU, memory and I/O virtualization hardware and software architecture
* Skilled in partnering and influencing architects, hardware engineers, and software leads
* Experience with gathering and analyzing system telemetry and low\\-level performance counters to identify and root\\-cause performance bottlenecks.
* Problem\\-solving skills, analytical capabilities, and attention to detail

 Diversity is a whole lot more than what we look like or where we come from, it’s how we think and who we are. We welcome people of all cultures, backgrounds, and experiences. But we’re not doing it single\\-handily: Our Kyndryl Inclusion Networks are only one of many ways we create a workplace where all Kyndryls can find and provide support and advice. This dedication to welcoming everyone into our company means that Kyndryl gives you – and everyone next to you – the ability to bring your whole self to work, individually and collectively, and support the activation of our equitable culture. That’s the Kyndryl Way.
   

  

**What You Can Expect**

 At Kyndryl, we design, build, manage and modernize the mission\\-critical technology systems that the world depends on every day. So why work at Kyndryl? **The Role**
 Are you passionate about solving complex problems? Do you thrive in a fast\\-paced environment? Then there’s a good chance you will love being a part of our Software Engineering – Development team at Kyndryl, where you will be able to see the immediate value of your work.
   

  

 As a Software Engineering \\- Developer at Kyndryl, you will be at the forefront of designing, developing, and implementing cutting\\-edge software solutions. Your work will play a critical role in our business offering, your code will deliver value to our customers faster than ever before, and your attention to detail and commitment to quality will be critical in ensuring the success of our products.
   

  

 Using design documentation and functional programming specifications, you will be responsible for implementing identified components. You will ensure that implemented components are appropriately documented, unit\\-tested, and ready for integration into the final product. You will have the opportunity to architect the solution, test the code, and deploy and build a CI/CD pipeline for it.
   

  

 As a valued member of our team, you will provide work estimates for assigned development work, and guide features, functional objectives, or technologies being built for interested parties. Your contributions will have a significant impact on our products' success, and you will be part of a team that is passionate about innovation, creativity, and excellence. Above all else, you will have the freedom to drive innovation and take ownership of your work while honing your problem\\-solving, collaboration, and automation skills. Together, we can make a difference in the world of cloud\\-based managed services.
   

  

 Your Future at Kyndryl
   

  

 The career path ahead is full of exciting opportunities to grow and advance within the job family. With dedication and hard work, you can climb the ladder to higher bands, achieving coveted positions such as Principal Engineer or Vice President of Software. These roles not only offer the chance to inspire and innovate, but also bring with them a sense of pride and accomplishment for having reached the pinnacle of your career in the software industry.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 You’re good at what you do and possess the required experience to prove it. However, equally as important – you have a growth mindset; keen to drive your own personal and professional development. You are customer\\-focused – someone who prioritizes customer success in their work. And finally, you’re open and borderless – naturally inclusive in how you work with others.
   

  

**Required Technical And Professional Experience**
* 7 years of experience working as a software engineer on complex software projects
* Excellent coding skills and solid development experience (Java, SpringBoot, Node.js, Express.js, React.js, Javascript etc) with debugging and problem\\-solving skills


 AWS
   

  

* Software development methodologies, with demonstrated experience developing scalable and robust software
* Experienced in relational and NoSQL databases, data mapping, XML/JSON, Rest based web services
* Knowledge of architecture design \\- Microservices architecture, containers (Docker \\& k8s), messaging queues
* Deep understanding of OOP and Design patterns


**Preferred Technical And Professional Experience**
* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, related technical field, or equivalent practical experience
* Certification in one or more of the hyperscalers (Azure, AWS, and Google GCP) \\- otherwise, you can obtain certifications with Kyndryl
* Experience with DevOps tools and modern engineering practices


**Being You**
 Diversity is a whole lot more than what we look like or where we come from, it’s how we think and who we are. We welcome people of all cultures, backgrounds, and experiences. But we’re not doing it single\\-handily: Our Kyndryl Inclusion Networks are only one of many ways we create a workplace where all Kyndryls can find and provide support and advice. This dedication to welcoming everyone into our company means that Kyndryl gives you – and everyone next to you – the ability to bring your whole self to work, individually and collectively, and support the activation of our equitable culture. That’s the Kyndryl Way.
   

  

**What You Can Expect**
 With state\\-of\\-the\\-art resources and Fortune 100 clients, every day is an opportunity to innovate, build new capabilities, new relationships, new processes, and new value. Kyndryl cares about your well\\-being and prides itself on offering benefits that give you choice, reflect the diversity of our employees and support you and your family through the moments that matter – wherever you are in your life journey. Our employee learning programs give you access to the best learning in the industry to receive certifications, including Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Skillsoft, and many more. Through our company\\-wide volunteering and giving platform, you can donate, start fundraisers, volunteer, and search over 2 million non\\-profit organizations. At Kyndryl, we invest heavily in you, we want you to succeed so that together, we will all succeed.
   

  

**Get Referred!**

description:
Couchbase, the operational data platform for AI, empowers businesses to succeed by bringing data to life in new ways. Major market\\-leading companies rely on Couchbase for mission critical operational, analytical, mobile and AI workloads. Built to replace legacy infrastructure and fragmented data services, Couchbase empowers enterprises with a unified platform architected for performance, flexibility and global scale.
   

  

 With Couchbase, organizations bring their data to life, launching game‑changing customer experiences, exploring the limitless potential of AI, and seamlessly extending applications from the cloud to the edge and beyond. Couchbase’s AI‑ready technology and enterprise partnership model eliminate complexity and reduce total cost of ownership, enabling teams to stay agile, innovative and secure.
   

  

 Couchbase believes data should never slow you down, but act as the foundation for your next breakthrough. Discover why Couchbase is trusted to help the world’s biggest players scale, move fast and stay resilient, no matter what’s next on their roadmap. Visit couchbase.com and follow us on LinkedIn and X.
   

  

 Want to be part of our story? Apply today!
   

  

 Lead Software Engineer \\- Storage
   

  

 As a key contributing member of the storage development team, you will be responsible for enhancing the highly scalable and performant storage engines used by both Couchbase Server and Couchbase Capella. You will be developing a highly\\-available and concurrent enterprise\\-grade system software. Most of all, you will be able to celebrate the wins by experiencing the direct result of your hard work from our customers’ success stories. The ideal candidate will have a strong technical background, excellent communication skills, and proactive problem\\-solving skills.
   

  

 The innovative work storage team does has been widely recognized by the industry. The following publications in VLDB conferences reflect the storage work at Couchbase
   

  

 Nitro: A Fast, Scalable In\\-Memory Storage Engine for NoSQL Global Secondary Index
   

  

 Magma: A High Data Density Storage Engine Used in Couchbase
   

  

**Requirements**
* Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in computer science or equivalent.
* 5\\+ years of experience building enterprise\\-grade infrastructure or systems software.

title: Engineer, Geotechnical
description:

 As a specialist/expert within the UK\\&IE based Geotech Team working predominantly on a range of variety of geotechnical projects across the UK world.

* Working in accordance with accepted methods and legislation (including CDM) and operate in accordance with WSP’s corporate health \\& safety, environment, and quality (SHEQ) standards. Taking ownership for aspects of SHEQ management across the geotechnical team.
* Undertake own continuing professional development and showing management of others across a team, to maintain and build professional capability.


 Key Competencies
   

  

 Mandatory Skills
   

  

* A sound understanding of geotechnical engineering, with a broad understanding of the underlying principles, and substantial project management experience of medium sized projects.
* Will have proven previous metro projects experience including undertaking under ground elements design, ground movement assessment, embedded retaining systems design, Diaphragm wall designs etc
* Will have proven previous experience of undertaking: geotechnical desk studies; critical checking of Ground Investigation Factual Reports; preparing Ground Investigation Reports in accordance with the requirements of Eurocode 7 : Part 1\\.
* Will have contributed to the production of some Geotechnical Design Reports.

This includes a demonstrable understanding of the underlying principles and a knowledge of Eurocode 7 and UK annex design codes, ground investigation and construction contracts and specifications.

* Proficiency with the use of geotechnical software for the management, review and presentation of ground investigation and geotechnical data using current best practice. Must have the skills to check, critically evaluate and amend your own work, be it drawings, calculations or reports.
* General commercial awareness and an understanding of client requirements as described in contracts, including an understanding and awareness of contractual issues and risks when dealing with clients and sub\\-consultants.
* Provides active contribution to technical excellence and innovation across the local geotechnical team
* Excellent report\\-writing and strong client liaison skills. Will have significant previous experience of undertaking and managing: geotechnical desk studies; designing, procuring \\& supervising Ground Investigations (GIs); checking and reviewing Ground Investigation Reports \\& Geotechnical Design Reports in accordance with the requirements of Eurocode 7 : Part 1\\. Client facing and able to lead at client/contractor meetings.
* Good oral communication skills also, able to provide solid support at client facing meetings and able to assist with client/contractor meetings.


**Desired Skills**
* Hands on experience in using the geotechnical software like XDISP, GEOSTUDIO, Repute 2\\.5, WALLAP, Settle 3D, Slide 2, Plaxis 2D and Plaxis 3D.
* Knowledge in the Engineering Geology or equivalent demonstrable experience.
* Chartered Engineer from a reputed professional body membership (preferably ICE, UK) or working towards chartered engineer.


**Qualifications**
* Master’s degree in civil engineering with Geotechnical Engineering / Soil Mechanics and Foundation engineering as specialisation.
* Working towards full professional membership / chartership.


 BGV:

title: Associate Director - Water Supply Modelling WS Pro
description:
**Responsibilities**
* Leads the technical delivery of a water supply network planning and modelling. Carries out hydraulic calculations, analysis and investigations for clients using Infoworks WS Pro software for modelling projects and tasks, including preparation of reports, figures and other documents to a high level of grammatical and technical presentation.
* Carries out project works in accordance with our Integrated Management System (IMS). Work alongside project managers and junior team members to ensure efficient delivery. Delivers through teams, not individually.
* Demonstrates industry best practice for model build, calibration and system performance assessments, including leakage and demand analysis, pressure management and infrastructure / growth planning.
* Demonstrates how Infoworks WS Pro models can be used to support the development of zone management plans, water supply strategies and demand management plans.
* Uses Infoworks WS Pro in\\-built tools to successfully investigate complex water network operational scenarios.
* Applies sound engineering judgement to resolve modelling anomalies to improve overall modelling confidence and communicates all risks and limitations on use of hydraulic models to the project team and the client.
* Works without supervision, direct others and ensures their work is verified by an appropriate professional.
* Leads continuous digital improvement and innovation on water hydraulic modelling and can identify and implement opportunities to streamline processes and data management.
* Successfully supports, coaches and mentors hydraulic modelling professionals to achieve a good standard of hydraulic modelling, embedding a culture of successful project delivery whilst also holding them to account, reducing re\\-work and supporting the continuous growth of the WSP water modelling capability.
* Ensures project related quality processes are carried out (technical reviews, verification, approval to release etc.) in a timely and cost\\-effectively manner.
* Undertakes appropriate verification of all inputs and outputs on hydraulic modelling projects including GIS, SCADA, field data and growth projections.
* Provides regular feedback to team members in a constructive and timely manner.
* Completes tasks within allocated timeframes and budgeted hours.
* Ensures all technical risks are understood, managed, assigned to the organisation best placed to manage the risk, and these are well communicated with the project team, and/or client
* Solves problems effectively by applying knowledge of hydraulics and engaging sound problem solving techniques and appropriate analysis to find value engineered/innovative solutions.
* Maintains knowledge of appropriate technical standards, legislation, codes of practice and New Zealand standards; Is knowledgeable on industry developments, trends, and processes.
* Applies sound judgement to decisions about technical issues and solves problems for clients to get the best possible outcome for a project.
* Undertakes appropriate verification of projects and team members’ work.
* Considering risks to client’s business or reputation as part of the planning and management of projects.
* Delivering excellent service to clients to promote repeat and uncompetitive work.
* Develops and maintains an extensive network of industry contacts in India and NZ (as a minimum) and is readily able to access key people.
* Builds and maintains strong, trusted relationships with clients.
* Actively seeks feedback from clients and communicate this to the team to ensure successful delivery of projects and lessons learnt.
* Ensure projects and variations are scoped and priced in a manner that will ensure commercial success and that respond to the specific risk profile of the project.
* Writes and supports bids that are commercially astute, recognises and manages commercial risks while demonstrating value to the client.
* Hold focused meetings that achieve appropriate outcomes at key times; Deliver engaging, informative well\\-organized presentations; Create opportunities to share learnings, improvements, new tools, better practices etc with the wider water team.
* Liaise and work closely with Sector leadership and peer groups across India and New Zealand; Act as a brand ambassador of GCC within WSP in India across geographies.


**Required Skills And Behaviours**
* Fluent in spoken and written English and able to present ideas, complex technical information and advice in a way that is understandable to a wide range of audiences; Communicate difficult/sensitive information tactfully.
* Able to work effectively and competently with people at all levels; within and external to the organisation. Ability to work collaboratively and demonstrated capability to perform effectively as part of a team across a matrix type structure.
* Models WSP Guiding Principles.
* Strong organisational ability to optimise and prioritise conflicting demands in a busy environment, to achieve deadlines and see responsibilities through to completion; copes well under conflicting pressures.
* Commercial and a level of maturity needed to understand business needs and drivers.
* Projects a credible image and inspires confidence; Excellent interpersonal skills.
* Able to manage others and support them through stressful or challenging situations.
* Results orientated, with a reputation for, and demonstrated achievement of, key strategic priorities.
* Active commitment to continuous personal improvement.
* A team player and considers the views of others.
* Ensures that confidentiality is respected and maintained at all times.
* Consistently demonstrates trustworthiness, integrity, professionalism and honesty.
* Natural and confident leader displaying openness, accountability, decisiveness and engagement; Client centric and passionate about service excellence .
* Displays drive, energy and enthusiasm for getting things done with high levels of motivation and energy; Project credibility demonstrate professionalism and inspire confidence with high emotional intelligence and self\\-awareness of strengths and potential areas for development.
* Excellent analytical skills and strong attention to detail.
* Resilient, comfortable with change and ambiguity.


 Software Proficiencies
   

  

* Must be highly proficient in WS Pro software for all water supply network planning applications.
* Highly capable of developing own SQL or other user\\-script applications for Infoworks WS Pro.
* Proficient use of Windows, MS Word/Excel and other Microsoft Office applications.


**Qualifications**
* Bachelors Degree in Civil Engineering followed by a Masters in Water Resource Engineering or Environmental Engineering or similar
* Professional Registration (Chartered Engineer status or equivalent).


**Experience**
* Minimum of 15 years’ experience post qualification in water supply network planning, engineering and hydraulic modelling.
* Must have at least 8 years’ experience using WS Pro software and extensive use within the last 12 months.
* Demonstrable experience in developing SQL or other user\\-script applications for Infoworks WS Pro.
* Past experience in NZ, Australian or UK projects is preferred but not essential.
